description: Gmail Deep Integration + Google Slides Integration: Backend API endpoints and frontend UI for scanning inbox to extract action items, and generating presentation outlines from task context.
steps: 1. Create backend endpoint POST /api/gmail/scan-inbox in backend/app/main.py (or a new router backend/app/api/gmail.py). It should: accept auth_token, use the Gmail MCP 'list_emails' + 'read_email' tools to fetch recent emails (last 10-20), then use Gemini to extract action items from email bodies. Return an array of suggested tasks with fields: email_id, email_subject, email_from, suggested_title, suggested_notes, and a source_email_id field for reply functionality.

2. Create backend endpoint POST /api/gmail/reply in backend/app/main.py or the gmail router. It should accept auth_token, email_id, and body text, then use the Gmail MCP 'send_email' tool (or direct API) to send a reply to that email thread.

3. Create backend endpoint POST /api/slides/generate-outline in backend/app/main.py or a new router backend/app/api/slides.py. It should: accept auth_token and task context (title, notes, subtasks), use Gemini to generate a presentation outline (title, slides array with title+bullets each), return the outline JSON.

4. Create backend endpoint POST /api/slides/create in backend/app/main.py or slides router. It should: accept auth_token and the outline data, use Google Slides API (google-api-python-client) to create a new presentation with the outline content. Return the presentation URL.

5. Add frontend API functions in frontend/lib/api-extended.ts: scanGmailInbox(authToken), replyToEmail(authToken, emailId, body), generateSlidesOutline(authToken, taskContext), createPresentation(authToken, outline).

6. Create frontend component frontend/components/gmail/GmailScanModal.tsx: A modal triggered by 'Scan Inbox' button. Shows loading state while scanning, then displays a list of extracted action items as cards. Each card has Accept (creates task) and Dismiss buttons. If a task was accepted and came from an email, show a 'Reply' button that opens a reply textarea.

7. Create frontend component frontend/components/slides/SlidesGeneratorModal.tsx: A modal triggered by 'Create Presentation' button in the task detail panel. Shows loading state while generating outline, displays the outline preview (editable slide titles/bullets), then a 'Create in Google Slides' button that calls the create endpoint and shows the resulting link.
8. Integrate GmailScanModal into the tasks page (frontend/app/dashboard/tasks/page.tsx) - add a 'Scan Inbox' button in the toolbar area.
9. Integrate SlidesGeneratorModal into the TaskDetailPanel in frontend/app/dashboard/tasks/page.tsx - add a 'Create Presentation' button in the detail panel actions area.
acceptance_criteria: Backend endpoint POST /api/gmail/scan-inbox compiles and returns structured action items from email content
Backend endpoint POST /api/slides/generate-outline compiles and returns a structured presentation outline
Backend endpoint POST /api/slides/create compiles and uses Google Slides API to create presentations
Frontend GmailScanModal component renders with scan trigger, loading, results, accept/dismiss/reply actions
Frontend SlidesGeneratorModal shows outline preview and create button
Scan Inbox button visible on tasks page toolbar
Create Presentation button visible in task detail panel
npm run build passes with no errors
